modules.name: geoarrow.c
documentation:
The root import for geoarrow. This import is intended for those working
with geoarrow at a low level; most users should use the pyarrow integration
as `import geoarrow.pyarrow as ga`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import geoarrow.c as ga
```

classes.name: geoarrow.c.lib.GeometryType
documentation:
Constants for geometry type. These values are the same as those used
in well-known binary (i.e, 0-7).

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import geoarrow.c as ga
>>> ga.GeometryType.MULTIPOINT
<GeoArrowGeometryType.GEOARROW_GEOMETRY_TYPE_MULTIPOINT: 4>
```

classes.name: geoarrow.c.lib.Dimensions
documentation:
Constants for dimensions.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import geoarrow.c as ga
>>> ga.Dimensions.XYZM
<GeoArrowDimensions.GEOARROW_DIMENSIONS_XYZM: 4>
```

classes.name: geoarrow.c.lib.CoordType
documentation:
Constants for coordinate type.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import geoarrow.c as ga
>>> ga.CoordType.INTERLEAVED
<GeoArrowCoordType.GEOARROW_COORD_TYPE_INTERLEAVED: 2>
```

classes.name: geoarrow.c.lib.EdgeType
documentation:

# Examples

classes.name: geoarrow.c.lib.CrsType
documentation:
Constants for coordinate reference system type.

# Examples
